text: The laparoscopic view shows the dissection of severe adhesions between the intestine and pelvic wall using monopolar electrocautery and scissors. The surgeon lifts the vagina by suturing to secure a better view of the pelvis, taking care not to damage the small intestine.

The surgeon moves the intestine cranially to expose the hernia orifice, measuring seven centimeters by seven centimeters. A three-centimeter margin is marked circumferentially around the orifice. The ventral right ST mesh is shaped and inserted into the abdomen through a port in the right lower quadrant. The mesh is fixed laterally and posteriorly using absorbable tacks, while the patency of the iliac arteries and veins is visually confirmed.
The surgeon sutures the mesh at the anterolateral side to prevent damage to the ureter, then expands the mesh to cover the pelvic floor. The laparoscopic procedure is completed.
